bbc.title: China's second typhoon in a week makes landfall
bbc.description: Nearly two million people are evacuated from eastern Zhejiang province, with the city of Wenzhou close to the path of the storm.

bbc.title: Iran supreme leader calls for revenge for father's killing
bbc.description: Mojtaba Khamenei says vengeance is "inevitable" for the killing, which happened on the first day of the US-Israeli war on Iran.

bbc.title: Spain battles to contain one of its deadliest wildfires as at least 12 killed
bbc.description: At least four Britons are believed to be among the victims of the blaze, which left another 23 people missing.

bbc.title: Trump administration subpoenas New York Times journalists over Air Force One reporting
bbc.description: The reporters received the legal summons after they reported on alleged security issues with the president's new plane, which was gifted by Qatar.

bbc.title: Polish PM pledges memorial to victims of WW2 'genocide by Ukrainian nationalists'
bbc.description: Warsaw and Kyiv have for decades been at loggerheads over the killings of many thousands of civilians during World War Two.

bbc.title: US Democrat Ro Khanna says he was detained by armed Israeli settlers
bbc.description: Khanna, 49, was visiting the occupied West Bank when he was held for 90 minutes, he says.

bbc.title: South Africa World Cup midfielder Adams dies aged 25
bbc.description: South Africa and Mamelodi Sundowns midfielder Jayden Adams dies at the age of 25, just weeks after featuring for his country at the World Cup.

bbc.title: More than 40 kidnapped children and teachers freed after Nigerian army operation
bbc.description: "I feel happy and elated… I feel joy," the head of the local teachers' union tells the BBC.

bbc.title: Will Trump Accounts deliver for American children?
bbc.description: The White House has hailed the project as giving new generations a stake in the American dream, but the scheme has its critics.

bbc.title: Major car firms found not to have installed emissions-cheating devices
bbc.description: A High Court judge said some major car manufacturers did not install a device to cheat emissions testing.

bbc.title: EasyJet agrees to surprise takeover bid as rival US firm swoops in
bbc.description: The airline says a bid from US firm Apollo has trumped a recent takeover offer from Castlelake.

bbc.title: 'Cool in 90 seconds' - the fake portable air conditioners sweeping the internet
bbc.description: The ASA says adverts claiming small devices can rapidly cool rooms were too good to be true.

bbc.title: Man nearly sucked out of window mid-air on Ryanair plane, passengers say
bbc.description: The man's wife held onto his legs for around five minutes to stop him from being sucked out, officials say.

bbc.title: Vapes to have less enticing names and flavours to protect children
bbc.description: People are being consulted about plans to stop vape companies using enticing flavour descriptions that "attract" children into experimenting.

bbc.title: Pressure builds on Europe's biggest port to be greener
bbc.description: A lawsuit demands that the Port of Rotterdam moves faster to cut its dependence on fossil fuel firms.

bbc.title: Chip giant SK Hynix raises $26.5bn as shares surge in bumper US listing
bbc.description: The shares surged as much as 17% in their Nasdaq trading debut on Friday following the record-breaking foreign listing.
